sourcesContent:
# composition matrix rows for single-line bodies: arrows in
# assignment-value, argument, callee, and return positions; then/else
# inline blocks at statement level and in switch arms.
double = (x) -> x * 2
lazy = -> 42
d = double(4)
r = ((x) -> x + 1)(5)
z = apply((x) => x * 2, 1)
def m()
  return (x) -> x

if d > 5 then big = 1
if d > 5 then big = 2 else big = 3
while d > 99 then d -= 1
label = ""
switch d
  when 8 then label = "eight"
  else label = "other"

# semicolons continue single-line bodies; parenthetical
# multi-statements are comma expressions in value position, bare blocks
# in statement position
chain = (x) -> x; inner = -> 2
if d then t1 = 1; t2 = 2
seq = (t1; t2)
if d then (t1; t2)
pick = -> (t1; t2)
